pr.title: lb: Match lbRefract, lbBgFlash, lbAudio, and lbColl functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Focused split from PR #2024 matching library-module functions in lbRefract, lbBgFlash, lbAudio, and reportedly lbColl. The PR replaced several anonymous fn_* placeholders with matched, typed implementations and clearer names, improved lbRefract_CallbackData field names, added local temporary structs for partially understood user data and texture pixels, fixed lbBgFlash_800205F0 to take a duration argument, and updated its gm_17C0 caller. Review emphasized keeping temporary local struct types instead of raw pointer arithmetic when user_data layout is not yet ready for a shared header.
what_changed: Matched and named several lbRefract helpers, including lbRefract_WriteTexCoordIA4 at 0x80021F34, lbRefract_ReadTexCoordRGBA8 at 0x80022120, and lbRefract_DObjDispReset at 0x80022608 in symbols.txt.

Implemented lbRefract_WriteTexCoordIA4 using a small local pixel struct with alpha and intensity fields, row_stride, tile_col, tile_base, and pixel_offset calculations for IA4 texture coordinate writes.
Implemented or clarified lbRefract_ReadTexCoordRGBA8 to compute RGBA8 texture component addresses from buffer, row, column, and row_stride, with nullable output pointers.
Renamed lbRefract_CallbackData fields in src/melee/lb/types.h from unk0-unk5 to buffer, format, width, height, row_stride, buffer_size, callback0, and callback1.
Updated lbRefract_8002219C parameters and internals to initialize named callback data fields and select callbacks based on GX texture format values 3, 4, and 6.
Matched lbRefract_80022560 as a framebuffer-to-refraction-source texture copy according to the PR body, and lbRefract_800225D4 as a TEV/indirect stage reset.
Renamed and matched lbRefract_DObjDispReset as a DObj display wrapper that calls hsdDObj.disp, resets TEV direct state, resets indirect stages, and invalidates HSD state.
Implemented lbBgFlash_800205F0 with a s32 duration parameter, clamps duration to at least 1, calls lbBgFlash_800206D4 with lbl_804D3848 and lbl_804D3840, and clears lbl_80433658.state.mode.
Updated lbBgFlash_800205F0 declaration in lbbgflash.h from void(void) to void(s32 duration) and updated gm_17C0.c caller to pass temp_r3.
Added BgFlashState and BgFlashData temporary structs in lbbgflash.c, replacing an older lbl_80433658_t bitfield placeholder with named active and mode fields and x4/x8/xC members.
Matched lbBgFlash_InitState at 0x8002087C, initializing active to 0, mode to 5, and xC from the duration pointer.
Implemented lbBgFlash_80021C48 using a temporary local struct containing padding and ColorOverlay x4, then calling lb_800144C8 on that overlay.
Changed lbBgFlash_80021A10 and fn_80021C18 to static where appropriate, removing lbBgFlash_80021A10 from the public header.
Renamed fn_800263B4 to lbAudioAx_ObjFree and documented it as freeing an object from the audio allocator pool via HSD_ObjFree when obj is non-NULL.
PR body says lbColl_80009DD4 was matched as debug quad drawing, but the provided changed_files and diff excerpt do not include an lbColl file, so details are not evidenced in this slice.
Added brief @file and @brief comments to lbrefract/lbbgflash source and headers.
smart_moves: Used semantic names based on concrete behavior rather than leaving matched code under fn_* names: WriteTexCoordIA4, ReadTexCoordRGBA8, DObjDispReset, InitState, and ObjFree.
Converted lbRefract_CallbackData from anonymous unk fields into operational field names, making row stride, format selection, buffer size, and callback wiring much easier to audit in later matches.
Preserved exact texture tiling/address math while improving readability with row_stride, tile_col, tile_base, and pixel_offset locals.
Handled minimum duration clamping in lbBgFlash_800205F0 and lbBgFlash_8002063C consistently, then fixed the known caller in gm_17C0.c after changing the function signature.
Kept partially understood layouts as local temporary structs rather than prematurely adding uncertain global header types.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt together with source renames, including local/global scope where shown, keeping symbol metadata aligned with decomp progress.
Used wrapper naming lbAudioAx_ObjFree to capture allocator intent while preserving the null-check and HSD_ObjFree call sequence.
decomp_lessons: When a function’s real behavior is understood, rename fn_* symbols and prototypes to behavior-based names and update symbols.txt in the same PR.
For texture buffer code, field names like buffer, format, width, height, row_stride, and buffer_size can make matching math readable without changing low-level aligned address calculations.
For GX texture formats, preserve numeric format cases when enum certainty is absent, but document known meanings such as 3=IA4, 4=IA8, and 6=RGBA8 if evidenced by code behavior.
Signature fixes must include all known callers; this PR changed lbBgFlash_800205F0 from void(void) to void(s32 duration) and updated gm_17C0.c accordingly.
Temporary local structs are acceptable for partially known embedded data layouts, especially when review expects future evidence before moving a type to a shared header.
Avoid raw pointer arithmetic on user_data when a temporary struct can express the known offset and type safely enough for current evidence.
Do not overgeneralize temporary structures into headers until repeated use confirms the layout; reviewers anticipated later usages might justify moving the type later.
Wrapper functions around engine allocators should keep null behavior explicit and name the allocator domain, as with lbAudioAx_ObjFree.
naming_conventions: Use module-prefixed names for understood library functions: lbRefract_*, lbBgFlash_*, lbAudioAx_*, lbColl_*.
Use action-object names for helpers: lbRefract_WriteTexCoordIA4, lbRefract_ReadTexCoordRGBA8, lbRefract_DObjDispReset, lbAudioAx_ObjFree.
Keep address-suffixed names such as lbRefract_8002219C or lbBgFlash_800205F0 when behavior is only partly known or public name is not established.
Use named struct fields instead of unkN when behavior is known: buffer, format, width, height, row_stride, buffer_size, active, mode.
Use static for local helpers that are not public API, such as lbBgFlash_80021A10, fn_80021C18, and lbRefract_DObjDispReset.
Header prototypes should reflect improved parameter names when known, such as lbBgFlash_800205F0(s32 duration), lbBgFlash_8002063C(int count), and lbAudioAx_ObjFree(void* obj).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Represented IA4 texture writes through a local two-byte pixel struct with alpha followed by intensity to avoid less-readable byte indexing while preserving layout.
Kept aligned texture row calculations explicit: IA4/IA8 row_stride uses (width * 8) & 0xFFFFFFE0, while RGBA8 uses (width * 0x10) & 0xFFFFFFC0.
Computed tiled texture addresses with col >> 2, row low bits, and column low bits, preserving the original bitmask forms such as 0xFFFFFFE0, 0xFFFFFFC0, and 0xC.
Used nullable output pointers in lbRefract_ReadTexCoordRGBA8 to match functions that optionally return component addresses.
Preserved a goto-style shared return path in lbRefract_8002219C switch cases, likely because it matched compiler control flow.
Maintained direct GX/HSD cleanup order in lbRefract_DObjDispReset: display DObj, GXSetTevDirect, GXSetNumIndStages(0), HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).
For uncertain object/user_data memory, used local anonymous structs with padding to access known fields at offsets instead of broad casts to incomplete global types.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Keep the temporary type for now rather than doing pointer arithmetic on user data; later repeated usage may justify moving the type to a header.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether similar pointer arithmetic in lbRefract_WriteTexCoordIA4 should use a local struct for alpha/intensity pixel layout.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Approved the local pointer arithmetic/local struct approach as fine for now.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move BgFlashGlobal or the ColorOverlay-containing user_data temporary struct into a shared header.
Search for other callers of lbBgFlash_800205F0 to confirm all duration arguments are typed and named consistently.
Inspect later lbColl changes to verify the lbColl_80009DD4 debug quad drawing match, since this slice lacks the lbColl diff.
Search for additional users of lbRefract_CallbackData callback0/callback1 to refine function pointer typedefs.
Check whether texture format numeric constants 3, 4, and 6 were later replaced with GXTexFmt enum names.
Look for subsequent renames of fn_80021F70, fn_80021FB4, fn_80021FF8, and fn_8002206C after the IA4/RGBA8 helpers were named.
